message:
fix(writing/wills-viz): tighten responsive breakpoints on TierTriage + WorkingFolder; drop broken wills URL suffix from diag spec

Two small follow-ups to the wills-and-estate-planning visualization work
that the recent copy/layout commits (4a3b24b, 0ad8720, 4d8ecdb) left on
the table.

TierTriageViz
- The ladder's connecting gradient line (left[10%]-right[10%] 1px bar
  across the top of the ladder) was gated on `md:block`, which made it
  appear at the md breakpoint even though the ladder itself was still
  collapsing into a cramped grid at md. Bumped to `lg:block` so the
  connector only shows up when the 5-column ladder is actually laid out.
- The ladder's grid was `grid md:grid-cols-5`, meaning it jumped from a
  single column straight to 5 columns at md — which is too tight on
  tablets and mid-sized laptops. Added `sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3`
  intermediate steps so the ladder grows 1 → 2 → 3 → 5 columns, and
  moved the final 5-column form to `lg` to match the connector gating
  above.

WorkingFolderViz
- Replaced the xl-only 5-track layout
  `xl:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto_minmax(0,0.92fr)_auto_minmax(0,1fr)]`
  with a plain `xl:grid-cols-3` (plus tighter `xl:gap-5`). The 5-track
  layout was reserving two auto columns for the mid-track "Ask" and
  "Wait ~30 min" indicators, which only made sense in horizontal flow.
- The indicator columns themselves are now hidden on xl (`xl:hidden`)
  since the three content tracks are now directly adjacent. Below xl
  the indicators still render as flow arrows between the vertically
  stacked sections, so mobile and tablet behavior is unchanged.
- Dropped `xl:rotate-0` from the ArrowRight icons inside those
  indicators; since the indicator wrappers are hidden on xl the icons
  never need to be rendered pointing right anymore, so the conditional
  rotation override was dead.

E2E diag spec
- `tests/e2e/_diag-wills-viz.spec.ts` was still pointing at the old
  URL slug `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning-using-ai`, which does
  not exist in `app/writing/` — the live route is
  `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`. Replaced the URL so the diag
  suite actually loads the page on all configured viewports instead of
  returning a 404 after 60s of networkidle waiting.

No component APIs, no test IDs, no page copy changed. Purely a
responsiveness fix + a broken test URL fix.
